variables: Variables are physical containers that store data like boxes
Variables can only hold one type of data (e.g., only numbers)
Changing a variable's value 'uses up' the old value forever (not understanding pointers/references)
loops: Loops always run an infinite number of times
Loops can only repeat the exact same action each time
A 'while' loop checks its condition constantly during the execution of the body, not just at the start of each iteration
algorithms: Algorithms are only for computers, not humans
An algorithm must be written in code to be valid
There is only one correct algorithm for any given problem
binary numbers: Binary is just another number system with no special meaning
Computers use binary because it's faster than decimal
Binary 10 is the same as decimal 10
data structures: Linked lists are always slower than arrays because they are 'linked'
Arrays can grow in size automatically without performance cost
A stack and a queue are basically the same thing because they both hold data
sorting: Bubble sort is the most efficient because it's the easiest to understand
Sorting only works on numbers, not text or complex objects
Quicksort is always $O(n \log n)$ regardless of the pivot choice
oop: An object is just a list of variables
Inheritance means the child class 'steals' code from the parent
Private variables are for security against hackers, not for encapsulation
recursion: Recursion is just a loop with a different name
A recursive function calls itself 'at the same time' (parallelism confusion)
Recursion will always eventually stop even without a base case

force: Force always causes motion (objects at rest have no forces)
Force is the same as energy
An object moving at a constant speed must have a net force acting on it
gravity: Gravity only affects objects on Earth
Heavier objects fall faster due to stronger gravity
There is no gravity in space (the 'vacuum' misconception)
energy: Energy is a physical substance that can be used up
Kinetic energy only comes from motion, not potential
Energy is 'lost' when a machine isn't 100% efficient (not realizing it's converted to heat)
thermodynamics: Cold is a substance that flows into objects
Heat and Temperature are the same thing
Entropy only applies to messy rooms, not closed systems
waves: Sound waves can travel through a vacuum like light
The medium (e.g., water) moves along with the wave
Waves 'die out' because they run out of energy (not understanding dissipation/attenuation)
electromagnetism: Electric current is used up by a lightbulb
Magnetic fields are just a different type of gravitational field
Battery 'stores' electrons (rather than providing the potential difference to move existing ones)

cells: All cells are identical regardless of function
Cells are just empty spaces filled with water
Plant cells have cell walls *instead* of cell membranes (they have both)
dna: DNA is only found in humans, not other organisms
DNA determines everything about an organism immediately
evolution: Evolution only happens to animals, not plants
Species evolve because they want to improve
Humans evolved *from* monkeys (rather than sharing a common ancestor)
cellular respiration: Respiration is the same as breathing
Cells only use oxygen for respiration
Photosynthesis and Respiration are 'opposites' that cancel each other out
cell division: Mitosis and Meiosis are the same thing but for different animals
Chromosomes only exist during cell division
Interphase is a 'resting' phase where nothing happens
circulatory system: The heart is the source of all 'feelings' and 'soul'
Arteries always carry oxygenated blood (forgetting the pulmonary artery)

fractions: Multiplying fractions always makes them larger
The larger the denominator, the larger the fraction
probability: Probability means something will definitely happen
Past events don't affect future probabilities (Gambler's Fallacy vs. Independent Events)
50% chance means if I do it twice, it will definitely happen once
geometry: Area and perimeter are the same thing
Shapes can only be measured in whole units
A square is not a rectangle
algebra: Variables are just placeholders with no meaning
Equations must always have a solution
You can't add $x$ and $y$ because they are different 'letters'
calculus: A derivative is just the 'slope' and nothing else
Integration is just finding the area under a curve
Infinity is a number you can reach
matrices: Matrices are just spreadsheets for storing data
A matrix with a zero determinant still has an inverse
